expressions.english: to change (one's clothes)
to wear clothes; to put on clothes
to wear pants; to put on pants
to wear pajamas; to put on pajamas
to wear (clothes) inside out
expressions.notes: 뒤집다 = to turn something inside out; to flip

expressions.english: Wear a suit/dress.
You look good in that outfit.
expressions.notes:
잘 = well
어울리다 = to suit, match

expressions.english: Can I try this on?
expressions.notes:
이 = this
-이/-이/여도 되다 = to be okay to + V

expressions.english: Have you decided what to wear?
Wear warmer clothes.
expressions.notes:
따뜻하다 = to be warm
따뜻하게 is the adverb form of 따뜻하다.

expressions.korean: 거기는 어떤 옷 입고 가야 돼요?
expressions.english: What kind of clothes do I have to wear when I go there?
expressions.notes:
거기 = there	가다 = to go; to leave
어떤 = what kind of	-이/-이/여야 되다 = to have to + V

expressions.english: I think you are wearing your t-shirt inside out.
expressions.notes:
티셔츠 = t-shirt
- 것 같다 = it seems/looks like + S + V

expressions.korean: 저기 노란 옷 입은 사람 누구예요?
expressions.english: Who's that person in the yellow shirt?
expressions.notes:
저기 = there
노랗다 = to be yellow
누란 is the adjective form of 노랗다.
누구 = who

expressions.korean: 이 옷 입어 봤는데 좀 큰 것 같아요.
expressions.english: I've just tried this on, but I think it's a little too big for me.
expressions.notes:
크다 = to be big; to be tall; to be loud
큼 is the adjective form of 크다.

expressions.korean: 지금 그 옷 입기에는 너무 덥지 않아요?
expressions.english: Isn't it too hot to wear those clothes?
expressions.notes:
지금 = now
-기에 = (to be too + adjective) + to + V
너무 = too much, excessively; very
덥다 = to be hot

expressions.korean: 입을 옷이 별로 없어서 옷 사러 가려고요.
expressions.english: I don't have many clothes to wear, so I am going shopping for clothes.
expressions.notes:
별로 = not particularly, not very, not so much, not really
사다 = to buy
-(으)는 가다 = to go to + V

expressions.korean: 한 번 입었는데 벌써 이렇게 더러워졌어요.
expressions.english: I've worn this just once and it already got dirty like this.
expressions.notes:
한 번 = once, one time
벌써 = already
더럽다 = to be dirty
-이/-이/여지다 = to become + adjective

expressions.korean: 지금 입고 있는 티셔츠는 어디에서 샀어요?
expressions.english: Where did you buy the t-shirt you are wearing now?

expressions.korean: 옷이 더러워져서 그대로는 못 입을 것 같아요.
expressions.english: My clothes got dirty, so I don't think I can wear them.
expressions.notes: 그대로 = as it is, without changing anything

expressions.korean: 인터넷 쇼핑몰에서는 옷을 사기 전에 입어 볼 수가 없어요.
expressions.english: On Internet shopping malls, you can't try on clothes before you buy them.
expressions.notes:
인터넷 = the Internet
쇼핑몰 = shopping mall
-기 전에 = before + S + V
